puzzles.title: Add Two Numbers
puzzles.prompt: Complete the program so it adds 5 and 7, storing the result in EAX.
puzzles.starter_code:
section .text
    global _start

_start:
    mov eax, 5
    ; TODO: add 7 to eax

    ; exit
    mov ebx, eax
    mov eax, 1
    int 0x80
puzzles.narrative: The void is quiet. Just you and the registers. You know what to do.
hints: ADD is the instruction for addition
ADD destination, source adds source into destination

puzzles.title: Print Hello
puzzles.prompt: Fix the program so it prints 'Hi' using Linux sys_write.
puzzles.starter_code:
section .data
    msg db "Hi", 0xA
    len equ $ - msg

section .text
    global _start

_start:
    ; TODO: write msg to stdout
    ; hint: eax=4, ebx=1, ecx=msg, edx=len

    ; exit
    mov eax, 1
    mov ebx, 0
    int 0x80
puzzles.narrative: You are speaking directly to the kernel now. No abstractions. No safety net.
hints: set eax=4, ebx=1 (stdout), ecx=msg address, edx=length

puzzles.title: Loop Sum
puzzles.prompt: Use a loop to sum the numbers 1 through 5 and store the result in EAX.
puzzles.starter_code:
section .text
    global _start

_start:
    mov eax, 0      ; sum
    mov ebx, 1      ; counter

loop_start:
    ; TODO: add ebx to eax
    ; TODO: increment ebx
    ; TODO: loop until ebx > 5

    jmp loop_start

end_loop:
    ; exit with eax as return code
    mov ebx, eax
    mov eax, 1
    int 0x80
puzzles.narrative: A loop. The most human thing in computing. Down here it is just jumps and flags.
puzzles.narrative_success: The void recedes. The flowers return. You are still here. Barely.
hints: add eax, ebx adds the counter to the sum
cmp ebx, 5 then jg end_loop exits when counter exceeds 5
